findings:
## Conductor/Worker Pattern Research: 9 Sources Across 6 Frameworks

### Coverage
AutoGen v0.4 Teams + Swarm, CrewAI, Semantic Kernel (overview + GroupChat), Google ADK (8 patterns), O'Reilly industry article (Osmani, Feb 2026), Sakana AI arXiv 2512.04388v4. LangGraph not scrapable (SPA redirect); covered via general knowledge.

---

### Per-Framework Summary

**LangGraph (supervisor pattern):** Supervisor LLM node routes via conditional edges. Workers return to supervisor after each step. Nesting via subgraphs allowed. Returns shared graph state dict. No explicit planning phase.

**AutoGen Teams (RoundRobin/Selector):** Turn-based team chat. RoundRobin = fixed rotation. SelectorGroupChat = LLM picks next speaker. Returns `TaskResult` (all messages + stop_reason). No planning phase.

**AutoGen Swarm:** DECENTRALIZED — agents use HandoffMessage to route to peers (no central conductor). All agents share broadcast context. Hub agents (e.g., Planner) orchestrate via back-and-forth handoffs, not central dispatch. Workers NOT strict leaf nodes — any agent can initiate handoffs.

**CrewAI:** Sequential (task list) or Hierarchical (manager LLM delegates). planning=True flag activates AgentPlanner that runs BEFORE execution — closest framework match to DevSteps R3 planning phase. Returns CrewOutput (raw, pydantic, JSON, tasks_output, token_usage). Hierarchical mode allows sub-crews (nesting).

**Semantic Kernel:** GroupChatOrchestration with GroupChatManager interface: SelectNextAgent(), ShouldTerminate(), FilterResults(). All patterns unified under orchestration.InvokeAsync() => OrchestrationResult<T>. Concurrent, Sequential, Handoff, GroupChat, Magentic presets available. No planning phase.

**Google ADK:** 8 patterns: Sequential (output_key => session.state), Coordinator (LLM routing via AutoFlow), ParallelAgent (concurrent threads, unique output_key prevents race conditions, synthesizer gathers), Hierarchical (AgentTool wraps sub-agent as callable), LoopAgent (retry/refinement with max_iterations and exit signal), HITL, Composite. Nesting explicitly supported at ALL levels. No typed return contract — results written to shared session.state dict.

**Sakana AI Conductor (arXiv 2512.04388v4, RL paper):** 7B Qwen2.5 Conductor trained via RL dispatches pool of frontier workers. Dispatch: Python lists (model_id, subtasks, access_list). Access_list drives topology: empty=parallel-independent, 'all'=sees all previous, indices=selective. Max 5 steps. Workers are STRICT LEAF NODES — no nesting. Conductor reviews output and can re-dispatch (recursive Conductor). Final worker output IS the result (no aggregation struct).

**O'Reilly Industry (Osmani, Feb 2026):** Conductor (synchronous 1-agent: Claude Code, Cursor inline) vs Orchestrator (async multi-agent: GitHub Copilot Agent, Jules, OpenAI Codex). Jules shows EXPLICIT plan-approval gate before async execution — confirms value of planning/execution separation. Conductor.build (Melty Labs) dispatches multiple Claude Code agents on isolated git worktrees in parallel.

---

### Comparative Dimensions

| Dimension | Best-in-class | DevSteps |
|---|---|---|
| Planning phase | CrewAI (flag), Jules (approval gate) | R3-exec-planner dedicated ring — AHEAD |
| Strict leaf nodes | Sakana AI Conductor | YES — matches academic best practice |
| Parallel dispatch | ADK ParallelAgent, Sakana access_list | NO — GAP |
| Return type | CrewOutput (typed), SK OrchestrationResult<T> | MandateResult (richest schema) — AHEAD |
| Retry/loop | ADK LoopAgent (embedded), recursive Conductor | External rejection-feedback — less integrated |
| Tier layers | Max 2 in all frameworks | 3-tier (coord=>conductor=>worker) — unique |
| Project integration | None | sprint_id + item_ids — unique |

---

### Architectural Validation

Strict leaf-node workers: VALIDATED by Sakana AI Conductor paper (2025 SOTA academic work).
Separate planning ring: VALIDATED by CrewAI and Jules; DevSteps has stronger architectural separation.
MandateResult schema: NO framework equivalent — unique DevSteps innovation with confidence/escalation/token_cost fields.

### Gaps Identified

1. No parallel worker dispatch — ADK ParallelAgent and Sakana tree topology show measurable throughput gains.
2. Retry loop is external to conductor — ADK LoopAgent and Sakana recursive Conductor embed this more cleanly.
3. Static compute allocation — Sakana Conductor dynamically sizes worker pool at dispatch time based on task complexity.
recommendations: Add parallel fan-out to exec conductors (ADK ParallelAgent pattern): dispatch N independent workers concurrently, gather MandateResults via Promise.all — highest-impact throughput gain.
Preserve and document R3-exec-planner as a differentiating architectural feature — only CrewAI's planning=True and Jules have analogous separation; DevSteps is ahead.
Consider embedding max-iteration retry loop within exec-conductors (ADK LoopAgent model) to reduce dependency on external rejection-feedback circuit for recoverable failures.
Publish MandateResult schema as an open typed return contract — no reviewed framework (AutoGen, CrewAI, SK, ADK) has an equivalent; unique DevSteps innovation worth documenting publicly.
Evaluate adaptive worker-pool sizing in exec-planner output: let R3 specify N workers and complexity hints per task (Sakana Conductor model), enabling conductors to right-size dispatch.
